creator: Anthony Ha
title: (No AI) Kimi: Threat or menace?
dc:creator: Anthony Ha
content: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."
contentSnippet: Chinese company Moonshot AI released a new version of its Kimi model this week, prompting concern about "full AI communism."

creator: Connie Loizos
title: (No AI) Neil Rimer thinks the AI money is coming back out
dc:creator: Connie Loizos
content: Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily.
contentSnippet: Neil Rimer, the venture capitalist who co-founded Index Ventures, predicts the historic wealth AI is generating in Silicon Valley will have to be redistributed, voluntarily or involuntarily.

title: (No AI) The apps, gadgets, and tools every reader needs
author: David Pierce
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/07/Installer-136.png?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

Hi, friends! Welcome to _Installer_ No. 136, your guide to the best and _Verge_\-iest stuff in the world. (If you're new here, welcome, hope your neighborhood isn't as smoky as mine, and also you can read all the old editions at the [_Installer_ homepage](https://www.theverge.com/installer-newsletter).)

This week, I've been recording the next season of [**_Version History_**](https://pod.link/1840983742) (this season's finale is out on Sunday!), reading about [**data center heists**](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/07/12/magazine/data-center-heist.html) and [**_Backyard Baseball_**](https://www.nytimes.com/2026/07/13/style/backyard-baseball-video-game-teacher.html?partner=slack&smid=sl-share&__readwiseLocation=) and [**the creator of** **_Calvin and Hobbes_**](https://therepublicofletters.substack.com/p/calvin-and-hobbes-and-the-price-of?__readwiseLocation=), canceling my October plans to see [**_Digger_**](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qORTe1wW3Wg) 30 or 40 times, taking on the [new Knockout Tour routes](https://www.nintendo.com/au/news-and-articles/mario-kart-world-update-adds-two-new-routes-to-knockout-tour-and-more/?srsltid=AfmBOorzvCLewxvYX4v9Vzght0LGU7akZC1DpTgmarrRX79wmvGkAsAy) in [**_Mario Kart World_**](https://www.nintendo.com/us/store/products/mario-kart-world-switch-2/?srsltid=AfmBOooYlHpV6SL5bZlSuyHL3kTL8SvgvUOrm11bmSclYqc86oxYL2r5), learning more than I ever intended about Staten Island thanks to [**_Revisionist His …_**](https://www.pushkin.fm/podcasts/revisionist-history/the-staten-island-problem-part-1-the-mayor-vs-the-borough-president)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/967544/best-apps-gadgets-reading-installer)

title: (No AI) TikTok is testing an AI likeness detection tool
author: Jay Peters
content:
![The TikTok logo on a black background with pink and blue repeating logos around the edges.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/23951408/STK051_VRG_Illo_N_Barclay_4_tiktok.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

TikTok is starting to test an opt-in tool that scans for AI likenesses and lets creators report them to the company, as spotted [by social media consultant Matt Navarra](https://x.com/MattNavarra/status/2078129989128450064). The tool is initially being tested with "some" US creators, TikTok US spokesperson Zachary Kizer tells _The Verge_. YouTube has been working on [a similar tool](https://www.theverge.com/news/803818/youtube-ai-likeness-detection-deepfake) and recently [made it available to all adult users](https://www.theverge.com/news/931884/youtube-likeness-detection-ai-deepfake-expansion-all-adults).

Creators who are part of TikTok's test and want to use the tool will first have to verify their identity with [a company called Jumio](https://www.jumio.com/about/). You'll have to do a real-time selfie scan and an ID check, but Kizer says that "TikTok does not retain ID documents, and facial informati …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/967486/tiktok-ai-likeness-detection-tool)

title: (No AI) A scorecard for the AI age

creator: David Nield
title: (No AI) How Google’s New Gemini Rates Work and How to Track Your Usage
dc:creator: David Nield
content: Now that Google has changed how its usage quotas are tallied, you might not get as many AI responses as you did before.
